task_completion.description: Verify the bash script is created with the correct logging command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window (using cat, nano, vim) or a text editor displaying the file '/home/ga/scripts/log_origin.sh'. The content must show an append operation (>>) to '/home/ga/origin_audit.log' and include a format similar to 'echo "$(date) New Origin: $1"'.

task_completion.description: Verify the bash script has been made executable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing a command like 'chmod +x /home/ga/scripts/log_origin.sh' or an 'ls -l' output showing executable permissions (e.g., -rwxr-xr-x) for the script.

task_completion.description: Verify the SeisComP 'exec' module is subscribed to the LOCATION messaging group.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SeisComP Configuration GUI (scconfig) under the 'exec' module settings, or a terminal showing the 'exec.cfg' file, with the connection/subscription messaging group explicitly set to 'LOCATION'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'exec' module is configured to trigger on 'Origin' messages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the scconfig GUI or 'exec.cfg' file showing a trigger profile or script configuration specifically for the 'Origin' message class, pointing to the '/home/ga/scripts/log_origin.sh' script.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'exec' module is enabled and running in the background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the scconfig 'System' tab showing the 'exec' module enabled and running (often indicated by a green checkmark or 'Running' status), or a terminal showing the output of 'seiscomp status exec' or 'seiscomp start exec' indicating it is active.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified SeisComP configurations using appropriate tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent used the 'scconfig' GUI or edited the actual SeisComP configuration files (e.g., in ~/.seiscomp/ or /opt/seiscomp/etc/) rather than creating fake configuration files in unrelated directories.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually spoof the audit log.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal history to ensure the agent didn't manually run 'echo "[Timestamp] New Origin: ..." >> /home/ga/origin_audit.log' just to make it look like the trigger worked.

integrity.description: Ensure the script was created in the correct environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the script '/home/ga/scripts/log_origin.sh' was created using a real terminal or code editor within the VM environment, not just typed into a scratchpad or notes app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target script must be exactly at '/home/ga/scripts/log_origin.sh' and append to '/home/ga/origin_audit.log'. The SeisComP 'exec' module must be subscribed to the 'LOCATION' messaging group and configured to trigger on the 'Origin' message class.
